contract_claim: The document references `LiteLLMProvider.complete()` twice as the location of the validation/retry loop. No `LiteLLMProvider` class exists in the codebase. The retry/validation loop is implemented in `DirectProvider.complete()` in `src/osoji/llm/_provider_base.py`. The `LiteLLMProvider` name appears to be from a prior architecture when litellm was used as the provider abstraction. All current providers (AnthropicProvider, ClaudeCodeProvider, GoogleProvider, OpenAIProvider, OpenRouterProvider) extend `DirectProvider`, which implements the shared tool-call retry and schema validation logic. [evidence: src\osoji\llm\_provider_base.py — "DirectProvider(LLMProvider) ... complete(messages, system, options) (L122-213) — main public entry point. Full async retry/validation loop"]
observed_behavior: Replace both occurrences of `LiteLLMProvider.complete()` with `DirectProvider.complete()` (from `src/osoji/llm/_provider_base.py`). Also update any surrounding prose that implies a single LiteLLM-based provider to describe the DirectProvider base class used by all concrete SDK providers.
